completed_tasks.name: Create backend venv, install packages, pre-download Whisper weights

remaining_tasks.name: Write bench_utils.py + smoke test + verify pytest green on OMEN-PC

decisions.decision: Use only OMEN-PC at 192.168.1.199 for Phase 0 host commands
decisions.rationale: The local Codex workstation is not the backend and produced misleading execution results
Persistent key material must not live in /tmp and this key is the verified backend path

decisions.decision: Write repo artifacts locally but validate backend-only behavior over SSH on OMEN-PC
decisions.rationale: The git checkout is local Linux while the executable Windows CUDA environment lives on OMEN-PC

next_action: Copy probes to OMEN-PC staging and run the backend smoke pytest with %USERPROFILE%\.venv-phase0\Scripts\python.exe
context_notes: SEV-1 process failure and user-trust incident. The user spent hours across days redoing SSH recovery because of wrong-machine execution, lost /tmp state, and high-friction speculative recovery. Future executors must treat these rules as blocking, not advisory.

incident.summary: Phase 0 execution was attempted on the wrong machine, persistent SSH state was lost by using /tmp, and the user was repeatedly forced into manual recovery loops.
incident.user_impact: Multiple days of setup churn, repeated copy/paste, invalid or overlong instructions, and justified extreme frustration.
root_causes: Wrong execution host was used despite research pointing at OMEN-PC
Persistent SSH material was treated as temporary
Remote success was claimed before enough command evidence was gathered
Recovery loops were longer and more speculative than necessary
prevention_rules: Use OMEN-PC only for Phase 0 backend work
Never store persistent SSH material in /tmp
Verify hostname, whoami, python --version, and nvidia-smi before claiming backend access works
Prefer the shortest validated recovery path under user frustration
